source: OWASP LLM Top 10 (2025), NIST AI RMF, Internal synthesis

risk_categories.name: Prompt Injection
risk_categories.description: Attacker crafts input that overrides system instructions or exfiltrates context
risk_categories.real_world_incident: Customer-facing chatbots manipulated to reveal internal pricing or bypass safety filters

risk_categories.name: Insecure Output Handling
risk_categories.description: LLM output passed to interpreters (shell, SQL, HTML) without sanitization
risk_categories.real_world_incident: Code-generation tools producing shell commands executed without review

risk_categories.name: Training Data Poisoning
risk_categories.description: Malicious data introduced into fine-tuning pipeline, altering model behavior
risk_categories.real_world_incident: Fine-tuned customer service models exhibiting unexpected brand-hostile responses

risk_categories.name: Model Denial of Service
risk_categories.description: Inputs crafted to maximize compute cost or cause timeouts
risk_categories.real_world_incident: Recursive prompt loops exhausting token budgets on pay-per-token APIs

risk_categories.name: Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
risk_categories.description: Risks from third-party models, plugins, or datasets
risk_categories.real_world_incident: Compromised MCP server returning malicious tool call responses

risk_categories.name: Sensitive Information Disclosure
risk_categories.description: PII, API keys, or confidential data exposed through model output or context
risk_categories.real_world_incident: System prompts containing API keys leaked via carefully crafted user queries

risk_categories.name: Insecure Plugin Design
risk_categories.description: LLM-triggered tool calls execute with insufficient authorization checks
risk_categories.real_world_incident: AI assistant given write access to production database via overpermissioned tool

risk_categories.name: Excessive Agency
risk_categories.description: LLM granted more permissions than required for its task
risk_categories.real_world_incident: Agentic system deleting files or sending emails without explicit human approval

risk_categories.name: Overreliance
risk_categories.description: Users or systems treating LLM output as ground truth without verification
risk_categories.real_world_incident: Legal teams citing AI-hallucinated case law in court filings

risk_categories.name: Model Theft
risk_categories.description: Extracting proprietary model weights or training data through API queries
risk_categories.real_world_incident: Fine-tuned models reconstructed via systematic API probing
